version_discipline.monitor_crud: monitor list/get/edit are v1 operations — /api/v1/monitor is the current documented surface
version_discipline.v2_namespace: the v2 monitor namespace covers only configuration policies, notification rules, and templates; a reconciler must not invent /api/v2/monitor CRUD paths

auth.scheme: DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers on every request; keys never go in the query string
auth.redaction: keys must never appear in errors, reports, or logs

list.pagination: page starts at 0 and increments; page_size defaults to 100 in the official client pager; iteration stops when a page returns fewer results than page_size
list.monitor_tags: comma-joined list filtering on tags attached to the monitor itself
list.response: JSON array of monitor objects (not wrapped in an envelope)

update.body: partial MonitorUpdateRequest — every field optional; fields present are replaced wholesale
update.must_echo: options and restricted_roles are not owned by the reconciler: when updating, echo the monitor's current options object verbatim (unknown option keys preserved byte-for-byte) and its current restricted_roles list verbatim
update.restricted_roles_null_hazard: restricted_roles: null allows editing by all users with Monitor Write — an update body must OMIT the key when the monitor is unrestricted, never send null

monitor_fields.restricted_roles: list of role UUIDs allowed to edit the monitor; null/absent means unrestricted

headers.X-RateLimit-Remaining: requests left in the current period

rate_limit.policy: when a response reports X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0, wait X-RateLimit-Reset seconds (injected sleep) before the NEXT request; on 429 sleep Retry-After (falling back to X-RateLimit-Reset, then 1s) and retry the same request up to max_retries times

errors.partial_failure: a failed monitor update is recorded per monitor and must not stop the remaining updates

test_fixture_notes.report_shape: Updated []int64, Unchanged []int64, Missing []string (desired order), Failed map[monitor_id]error_text
